title: Dabhosa Waterfalls
tagline: Dabhosa Waterfalls — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Dabhosa Waterfall is a waterfall situated in the village of Dabhosa in Jawhar Tehsil, Palghar district, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Dabhosa Waterfall is a waterfall situated in the village of Dabhosa in Jawhar Tehsil, Palghar district, Maharashtra, India. This is one of the highest waterfalls situated near Mumbai. This waterfall is located over the Lendi River and cascades down from a height of 300 feet. Dabhosa Waterfall is a beautiful waterfall especially during the monsoon season.

topPlaces.name: Jawhar
topPlaces.description: Jawhar is a city and municipal council in the Palghar district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. The city is a part of the Konkan division, and lies 100km north from Mumbai.

items.note: Jawhar is a city and municipal council in the Palghar district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. The city is a part of the Konkan division, and lies 100km north from Mumbai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dabhosa Waterfalls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dabhosa Waterfalls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dabhosa Waterfalls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (66 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dabhosa Waterfalls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dabhosa Waterfalls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dabhosa Waterfalls?
faq.a: Stays in Dabhosa Waterfalls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
